query: DynamoDB write throughput scalability 50000 events per second 2024
answer: DynamoDB can scale to support up to 50,000 write events per second using on-demand capacity mode, which automatically adapts to traffic. As of 2024, DynamoDB allows configuring maximum throughput for on-demand tables. DynamoDB's provisioned mode offers burst capacity for spiky workloads.

results.title: DynamoDB Throughput Capacity Modes | by Joud W. Awad - Medium
results.snippet: While DynamoDB can keep scaling to support virtually unlimited throughput, each individual partition is limited to 1,000 write units per second

results.snippet: For example, if your previous peak was 50,000 reads per second, DynamoDB instantly handles up to 100,000 reads per second. Once you sustain that

results.title: Amazon DynamoDB introduces configurable maximum throughput ...
results.snippet: Starting today, you can optionally configure maximum read or write (or both) throughput for individual on-demand tables and associated secondary indexes.

results.title: how Amazon DynamoDB handles spiky workloads in short intervals | AWS re:Post
results.snippet: When you use provisioned mode, DynamoDB provides a degree of flexibility in your per-partition throughput provisioning by providing burst capacity. DynamoDB currently retains up to 5 minutes (300 seconds) of unused read and write capacity. Suppose that a DynamoDB table has its capacity provisioned at 150 RCUs and has no usage in the last 5 minutes. For this configuration, the table has 45,000 read units of burst capacity (150 \* 300). Therefore, if a sudden spiky load pushes consumption to 200 read units, then DynamoDB can leverage burst capacity to accommodate 200 RCUs for 15 minutes. In on-demand capacity mode, DynamoDB automatically adapts to your application's traffic volume. On-demand capacity mode instantly accommodates up to double the previous peak traffic on a table. In this case, the on-demand capacity mode instantly accommodates sustained traffic of up to 100,000 reads per second. * Why is my DynamoDB provisioned table throttled? * How do I change the read and write capacity mode for my DynamoDB table?

results.title: DynamoDB Deep Dive: Scaling and Performance - AWS
results.snippet: Amrith Kumar, a Senior Principal Engineer at AWS, explains how DynamoDB handles massive workloads of up to half a million requests per second
